description: Fix stream resource leak and missing error handler in the CLI start command that can crash the process or leak reader locks.

userStories.title: Fix teeStream reader lock leak and add logStream error handler
userStories.description: As a CLI user, I want opentabs start to handle stream errors gracefully so that write failures don't leak reader locks and missing logStream error handlers don't crash the process.
acceptanceCriteria: teeStream releases the reader lock (via reader.releaseLock() or reader.cancel()) if terminal.write or logFile.write throws
logStream (createWriteStream) has an error event listener that prevents uncaught error crashes
The error listener logs a warning (console.warn or similar) so the user knows log writing failed
Normal (non-error) teeStream behavior is unchanged — stdout and stderr still appear on both terminal and log file
userStories.notes:
File: platform/cli/src/commands/start.ts

Issue 1 — teeStream (lines 94-106): The reader from readable.getReader() acquires a lock on the ReadableStream. If terminal.write(value) or logFile.write(value) throws, the for-loop exits without calling reader.releaseLock(), leaking the lock. Wrap the loop body in try-finally or try-catch to ensure the reader lock is released on error. Consider using reader.cancel() in the error path which both releases the lock and signals the source.

Issue 2 — logStream (line 242): createWriteStream('...', { flags: 'a' }) returns a WriteStream. Node.js streams that emit 'error' without a listener cause an uncaught exception that crashes the process. Add logStream.on('error', ...) before using it. A reasonable handler: log a warning to stderr (console.warn) and continue — log file failure shouldn't kill the server.
